mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding corporate output capacity and improving profit margins, which directly supports higher equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO momentum is negative over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are lower when INDPRO rises.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, consumer spending power collapses and recession fears dominate market sentiment, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising consumer prices and a stagnant federal funds rate creates an environment of negative real interest rates, which boosts equity valuations as investors seek yield in risk assets over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising CPIAUCSL and flat FEDFUNDS yields a negative Rank IC over a 5-year sample, or forward returns are not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, simultaneous increases in housing starts and retail sales signal robust consumer demand and economic stabilization, leading to positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In post-shock recovery periods, concurrent rises in HOUST and RSAFS are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in over 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining inflation readings reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings and alleviate fears of aggressive central bank tightening, generating positive forward equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative when CPIAUCSL declines, with a Rank IC below -0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200,000 signals strong labor market expansion and rising wage pressures, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 200,000.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates while industrial production remains stable, it indicates a structural shift toward services and financial sectors, which supports S&P 500 performance over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction of falling GDPC1 momentum and flat INDPRO yields negative 60-day forward returns in over 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, rising federal funds rates directly increase borrowing costs for corporations and reduce the present value of equity cash flows, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: During tightening cycles, rising FEDFUNDS is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or Rank IC is positive.
